text:
Once there was a fish named Felix.

Felix lived his life swimming gracefully, finding food to eat, resting on sunny beaches, hiding from predators, and occasionally sunbathing on coral reefs. Though he was a small fish, he had a big personality.

One summer, while lazily drifting across the coral reefs, Felix heard strange buzzing sounds coming from beneath the waves. Curiously, he swam closer. That's when he saw them—the enormous bees!

These were no ordinary bees. They were huge!

Felix decided to hide. He zipped underwater behind a rock. But these wasn't ordinary bees. They were hummingbirds!

You see, huge hummingbirds lived deep underwater. They were fascinated by colorful fish like Felix. With a quick wiggle, Felix swirled through a school until he found something even more awe-inspiring.

It was a blooming coral headfeather—the largest I've ever seen. It was as bright as a sunbeam.

You could scarcely believe
